If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: Express Donate: $50 Express Donate: Other Amount Express Donate: $20 Express Donate: $10 My name is Omar Fateh  Im a State Senator, husband, renter, son of Somali immigrants, and a long-time advocate for working people.  Im running for Mayor of Minneapolis to work with you to achieve the vibrant, loving city we know Minneapolis can be. For working people, its getting harder to build our lives in Minneapolis. And with Donald Trump stepping back into the Oval Office, everything weve worked so hard to create is in jeopardy.   It's not enough to just stand up to Donald Trump.  Its time to push back with forward-thinking leadership. Mayor Frey and the status quo are failing Minneapolis residents.  We deserve leadership that makes it so people want to continue to live here, raise families here, and start businesses here. Minneapolis must be a north star for our nation in a dark time. We can show the country what happens when we invest in people, not corporate special interests. Block by block, were already building the city we want to see.  Its time we had a Mayor willing to work alongside us. Im committed to building a city that works for everyone. Serving in the Minnesota Senate,  Ive seen firsthand what a progressive legislature can do with the right executive.  As Mayor, I will work with the progressive City Council to achieve tangible wins for our neighbors and uplift those of us who are struggling to get by. Jacob Frey first ran for Mayor seven years ago with a promise to end homelessness by 2022, and more people are living without a home today as a result of his failed policies. Its been over four years since MPD Officer Derek Chauvin murdered George Floyd, and he still hasnt delivered on a vision for public safety. He vetoes every solution that comes to his desk.  Its time for a Mayor who has real solutions that puts Minneapolis residents first. Ive worked on a range of issues in the State Senate that directly meet the needs of people in our communities:   funding free college tuition for tens of thousands of Minnesotans securing a statewide living wage for Uber and Lyft drivers investing $19 million dollars for public safety in Minneapolis saving the 2040 Plan to make sure we are building more affordable housing and ensuring a cleaner environment for all of us via the 100% Clean Energy Bill.  Now, Im ready to bring that leadership to City Hall. We need a Mayor who understands that a city that lifts up working people is a city that lifts up everyone. Because when the city invests in us, we have more time to invest in the people we love.  From the streets to City Hall, Minneapolis is full of people who are ready to invest in long-term solutions that will bring security and stability to our communities.  Minneapolis residents work hard for the city we love. We deserve a Mayor that works as hard as we do. Donate Mayor Frey and the status quo are failing Minneapolis residents.  We deserve leadership that makes it so people want to continue to live here, raise families here, and start businesses here. LOUIS I am the People’s Mayor,   and I believe that no matter where in our city you call home, you should have the opportunity and resources to succeed. progressive means progress, so that every St. Louisan has more support, safety, and equal access to quality city services. I believe in showing up for our city and fighting for our values, whether we need a resolute champion or a passionate defender. OUR PLATFORM When I ran for Mayor, I had countless conversations with voters about the challenges St. Louis faces: long-term decline in our economic might, population growth, and in many basic quality-of-life city services. I pledged myself to tackling each and every one of those challenges as Mayor.  It isn’t easy to turn around decades of neglect and dysfunction—but it is necessary and crucial work that we have made real progress on: New approaches to public safety have successfully lead to a major drop in crime, including a double-digit decline in our city’s homicide rate. We put protections in place for our residents against dangerous, hateful laws passed by extremists in the Missouri legislature. We directed funding towards new programs to lift up our youth and bring dignified jobs to underserved areas. We are working to rebuild our city government, modernizing processes and exploring new technology to let us serve you with efficiency. Improvements to public transit, strengthening tenant rights, and common sense gun control policies have also made our people safer, our city more accessible, and our future brighter. We have gotten so much closer to achieving the vision of a thriving St. Louis that we all know is possible.  You and I know that our work is far from over, but we are making true progress.  I have always been, and will always be, dedicated to building an equitable and prosperous St. Louis for everyone that calls our city home. God's Word tells us, “If My people who are called by My name will humble themselves, and pray and seek My face, and turn from their wicked ways, then I will hear from heaven, and will forgive their sin and heal their land.” - ‭‭II Chronicles‬ ‭7‬:‭14‬ Pray. Plan. Proceed. The Platform 1.  Safe City Establish partnerships between law enforcement, the faith community, community leaders, social services, and residents. This collaboration will enhance trust and reinforce the message that violence is unacceptable to the entire community. Implement strategies which employ a focused approached to identify the small number of individuals and groups most responsible for gun violence and address through direct intervention. Authorities and community partners meet directly with offenders to convey consequences of continued violence and offer assistance for those who want to change. Read Less Read More 2.  Restore Infrastructure Establish a close working relationship with Jxn Water to help ensure we have a voice in the direction of and future management of these vital systems. Manage street improvements through a comprehensive pavement management program shared with government partners to maximize benefits received from city and county tax dollars, One Percent Sales Tax Commission resources, State Aid Road funds, and Capital Complex Improvement District investment. Develop a traffic control device strategy which will determine appropriate traffic control devices needed based on traffic volume, crash history, and pedestrian activity to prioritize repair, replacement or removal based on these criteria. Read Less Read More 3.  Job Creation & Training Equip residents with skills aligned with the needs of local industry by partnering with local business, non-profits, and education institutions to offer work skill mentoring, apprenticeships, and career readiness programs. Encourage public-private partnerships for collaboration between private investors, nonprofits, and education institutions to pool resources and incentivize investment in underserved areas. Work with state and federal partners to evaluate and implement programs successfully utilized in other states providing residents opportunities to transition into the workforce without experiencing the benefit cliff. Read Less Read More 4.  Beautification & Blight Elimination Encourage and support grassroot efforts to identify blight and organize neighborhood cleanup or beautification projects through neighborhood associations and residents. Collaborate with developers and nonprofit organizations to fund and manage blight removal and redevelopment efforts such as incremental infill development strategies successfully implemented in other cities. Work with our Legislative delegation to streamline processes that currently hinder cleanup of distressed and tax foreclosed properties providing avenues to repurpose and redevelop properties. Read Less Read More 5.  Home Ownership Opportunities Work with local financial institutions and non-profits to offer workshops and other resources on budgeting, mortgage options, and maintaining good credit to prepare individuals for successful homeownership. Establish programs to sell vacant, tax foreclosed or blighted properties at low cost to first time home buyers or residents currently renting and require the property be renovated and owner occupied. Provide incentives for first time home buyers to acquire and rehabilitate tax foreclosed properties. This approach makes homeownership more accessible, improves vacant properties, and returns properties to the tax rolls. Read Less Read More About Rodney In Brief Jackson resident from 1966 – 1976 and 2004 – current Education:  B.S. in Business Administration – Accounting Public Office:  Elected to Clinton Board of Alderman in 1993, served from 1993 – 2001 Career:  Owned and operated DePriest Construction for 20+ years; Senior Management, Trustmark National Bank from 2012 – 2021 Community Service:  Founded REJXN in 2023 with an emphasis on violence intervention; Young Life Inner City Jackson Volunteer Served on the Board of Directors for Downtown Jackson Partners, 2020-21 Member and Deacon at First Baptist Church Jackson Learn More I need your support. Donate, volunteer, host events and spread the word. Any effort helps! Volunteer Donate Copyright © 2025 - Rodney DePriest for Mayor. All rights reserved

DONATE VOLUNTEER Our People. Our Places. Our Prosperity. Building A Stronger Helena & Moving Our Community Forward The People of Helena My primary focus is you.  I am here to listen to  your insights, address your concerns, and understand your challenges. As your City Commissioner, I am committed to showing up for you and our community. We are in the people business and our democracy is stronger when you're a part of it.  Please do not hesitate to reach out. A Strong Economy Helena's small businesses are the heart and soul of our community. Helena's business owners not only employ the majority of our friends and neighbors, they also give back to our town in ways we cannot calculate. ​ I have worked directly with Helena's Chamber, Business Improvement District, and Businesses to find solutions that remove barriers and strengthen our economy I am committed to helping businesses start, operate, and grow in Helena.  Affordable Housing Housing is a top priority for me. I recognize the urgency of the issue and I am dedicated to the idea that building more affordable housing only strengthens our community. ​ Housing will help with our workforce crisis, helping to retain and attract families to the area. I am honored to have joined my fellow commissioners in  approving over 1,500 units  &  investing millions into affordable housing . Reliable Infrastructure Roads, sidewalks, bridges,   water, and sewer  might not seem that exciting, but they are the foundations of our community. ​ The city has invested in a new Ten Mile water treatment plant, and the crosstown connecter, further investing in our public health and water security.   See infrastructure projects. ​ Roads, sidewalks, and sewers, we must continue to invest, maintain, and secure our infrastructure and sustainability A Safe Community I am committed to keeping Helena a safe community by investing in our Fire Department, Police Department, 911 dispatch, and the relationship between the SROs and Helena's Public Schools. ​ A safer community is more than just equipment it's also about training, and improving communication and coordination. ​ This is why I supported and worked to build a third fire station, and hire more firefighters, police officers, and detectives.  ​ A Balanced Approach I am committed to a balanced approach to government, by living within our means, cutting needless regulations and fees, and investing it back into the community when there is a little extra. A prime example is when the city invested its ARPA funding towards affordable housing, a food resource center, and fire education. ​ See our budget here. Our Parks & Open Spaces Helena's parks and open spaces are not only vital to our quality of life and our work/life balance, but our 70 miles worth of trails, city parks, our city pool, Bill Roberts Golf Course, and the civic center bring visitors from all over the world to recreate and enjoy our outdoors, as well as spend money on lodging, dining, and at many of our small businesses. ​ I am committed to protecting access to our public lands and open spaces.  Strategic Partnerships The city may not build housing, but we can update zoning, remove barriers, and approve developments. We might not feed people, but we can invest in programs and organizations that do just that. The city can do a lot of things, but we can do them better with the help of our strategic partners.  ​​​ ​ Public Service As Winston Churchill once said, " We make a living by what we get, but we make a life by what we give." ​ There are many ways to serve our community like volunteering to help those in need,  donating funds to a cause you believe in ,  registering friends and neighbors to vote,  and  giving blood and plasma. ​ The United Way of Lewis and Clark has a page full of volunteer opportunities.  Historic Preservation Since the appointment process and my first interview after becoming a City Commissioner, I have committed to,  "protecting and preserving,"  our community's rich history. By reinvesting  Tax Increment Finance (TIF)  funds into our historic downtown, revitalizing  historic buildings , and  restoring the Firetower , I will continually strive to protect, preserve, and celebrate our heritage. I am honored to have joined my fellow commissioners in  approving over 1,500 units  &  investing millions into affordable housing . Reliable Infrastructure Roads, sidewalks, bridges,   water, and sewer  might not seem that exciting, but they are the foundations of our community.  The city has invested in a new Ten Mile water treatment plant, and the crosstown connecter, further investing in our public health and water security.   See infrastructure projects.  Roads, sidewalks, and sewers, we must continue to invest, maintain, and secure our infrastructure and sustainability A Safe Community I am committed to keeping Helena a safe community by investing in our Fire Department, Police Department, 911 dispatch, and the relationship between the SROs and Helena's Public Schools.  A safer community is more than just equipment it's also about training, and improving communication and coordination.  This is why I supported and worked to build a third fire station, and hire more firefighters, police officers, and detectives.   A Balanced Approach I am committed to a balanced approach to government, by living within our means, cutting needless regulations and fees, and investing it back into the community when there is a little extra. A prime example is when the city invested its ARPA funding towards affordable housing, a food resource center, and fire education.  See our budget here. Our Parks & Open Spaces Helena's parks and open spaces are not only vital to our quality of life and our worklife balance, but our 70 miles worth of trails, city parks, our city pool, Bill Roberts Golf Course, and the civic center bring visitors from all over the world to recreate and enjoy our outdoors, as well as spend money on lodging, dining, and at many of our small businesses.  I am committed to protecting access to our public lands and open spaces.  Strategic Partnerships The city may not build housing, but we can update zoning, remove barriers, and approve developments. We might not feed people, but we can invest in programs and organizations that do just that. The city can do a lot of things, but we can do them better with the help of our strategic partners.    Public Service As Winston Churchill once said, " We make a living by what we get, but we make a life by what we give."  There are many ways to serve our community like volunteering to help those in need,  donating funds to a cause you believe in ,  registering friends and neighbors to vote,  and  giving blood and plasma.  The United Way of Lewis and Clark has a page full of volunteer opportunities.  Historic Preservation Since the appointment process and my first interview after becoming a City Commissioner, I have committed to,  "protecting and preserving,"  our community's rich history. By reinvesting  Tax Increment Finance (TIF)  funds into our historic downtown, revitalizing  historic buildings , and  restoring the Firetower , I will continually strive to protect, preserve, and celebrate our heritage. She points to how she's handled public safety."Omaha definitely is a safe city, and I love talking to people from out of town, especially like during the College World Series. And the two things I hear the most is, 'I feel so safe here.' And 'It's so clean here,'" Stothert said.In 2024, she notes a 21% reduction in violent crime and 19% reduction in crime overall. Stothert also touts Omaha having its lowest homicide rate in 30 years."The city is definitely so much safer than it was before," she said.In a contentious race, she stands by the streetcar project despite some pushback."Mike McDonnell is being very disingenuous and misleading the voters because he can't stop it. He can't override 19 different votes that the Omaha City Council voted on and approved. He can't override It. He can't stop it at this point in time," Stothert said.The mayor said she's aware of what she calls online rumors that she's never actually working in Omaha. His leadership as Douglas County Treasurer and his deep roots in local community service highlight his readiness to serve as your next Mayor. Join Our Campaign Request a Yard Sign CLICK HERE TO EMAIL ALL PRESS & MEDIA INQUIRIES OMAHA IS HOME John with His Loved Ones Honoring Excellence and Service 2021  Omaha Press Club Face on the Wall 2020 Nominated for County Official of the Year 2018 The Dean Hubert Locke Award for Excellence in Public Service UNO College of Public Affairs and Community Service 2014  University of Omaha Alumni Association Citation for Career Achievement 2011 National Association of County Officials Award for the Treasurer’s Business Model 2010  College of Public Affairs and Community Service Excellence in Public Service Award 2010  NAACP Living Legend Award 2007 Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. Living The Dream Award 2007  Midlands Chapter of the National Management Association Manager of the Year 2006  Domestic Violence Coordinating Council Purple Ribbon Award JOHN IS A 43 YEAR PUBLIC SERVANT WITH PURPOSE John W. Ewing, Jr.  A Lifetime of Service REASONS 0 Why You Should Vote for John Ewing Jr. for Omaha Mayor John Ewing Jr. has a proven track record of service, leadership, and innovation. As a candidate for mayor, he brings nearly 25 years of experience in law enforcement, 18 years of leadership as Douglas County Treasurer, and a deep commitment to the safety, well-being, and growth of our community. Here are 43 compelling reasons why John Ewing Jr. is the right choice for Omaha’s future: Support Our Campaign 1. Decades of Service John Ewing Jr. served nearly 25 years in the Omaha Police Department, rising to the rank of Deputy Police Chief. He understands the importance of safety and effective leadership. 2. Neighborhood Safety Advocate As a former police officer and a long-time public servant, John has always championed the cause of neighborhood safety, ensuring our communities are secure for everyone. 3. Experienced Treasurer John has served as Douglas County Treasurer for 18 years, successfully managing the county’s finances with integrity and transparency. 4. Proven Fiscal Responsibility He has kept his campaign promises, balancing the county’s budget to the penny each month, restoring fiscal integrity, and eliminating embezzlement within his office. 5. Technology for Efficiency John has led the charge to implement technology that makes government services more accessible, like online property tax payments and phone payment options—free to the people of Douglas County. 6. Innovative Solutions As Treasurer, John worked with state and local partners to introduce online vehicle registration and payment services, further streamlining processes and saving residents time and money. 7. Leadership in Victim Advocacy As Commander of the Special Victims Unit, John spearheaded efforts to pass crucial legislation (LB 943) that improved protections for women and children in Nebraska. 8. Investment Expertise John implemented an Investment Committee that generated millions of dollars for Douglas County, ensuring taxpayer dollars are working harder for the community. 9. Generating Revenue for the County Through his creation of the Dealer Center, John has generated over $7 million annually in fees that directly benefit Douglas County. 10. Boosting County Income His efforts have increased county revenues through smart investments, bank account management, and commissions, totaling more than $90 million per year. 11. Crime Prevention Leadership As Deputy Chief, John co-chaired the Crime Prevention Covenant, which led to Omaha’s 360 Program—a groundbreaking initiative that has reduced gun violence by 70%. 12. National Recognition for Crime Reduction Under John’s leadership, Omaha has been recognized as a Model Community by President Barack Obama’s Foundation for its efforts to reduce crime. 13. Empowerment Network Leadership John has served as Board President and Vice-President of the Empowerment Network, helping shape policies that empower our community. 14. Community Servant With over 70 board and committee memberships, including with Habitat for Humanity and the Charles Drew Health Center, John has demonstrated his commitment to improving Omaha’s quality of life. 15. Effective Fundraising John chaired the Von Maur Victims Fund, a successful initiative that became a national model for victim assistance programs. 16. United Way Leadership As Chair of the United Way’s Planning and Allocation Committee, John has led efforts to address Omaha’s most pressing needs. 17. Homeland Security Expertise John led the Urban Area Security Initiative, securing $13 million in federal funding to protect Omaha and surrounding counties after 9/11. 18. Health Care Champion As Chair of a Federally Qualified Health Center board, John has been a vocal advocate for healthcare as a fundamental right. 19. Economic Development Vision John is committed to reinvigorating Omaha’s economic development plan, focusing on programs that create living-wage jobs and reduce poverty. 20. People Over Politics John will prioritize the needs of Omaha’s residents over special interest groups, ensuring that the people of Omaha always come first. 21. Transparency in Government John will provide transparency on major projects, ensuring citizens have a voice in decisions like the streetcar project. 22. Balanced Development John will focus on increasing development opportunities through creative solutions, avoiding over-reliance on Tax Increment Financing (TIF). 23. Proven Experience in Public Safety With extensive experience in recruiting and retaining police officers, John is committed to keeping Omaha’s neighborhoods safe. 24. Innovative Government John has consistently introduced new technologies and systems to make government more efficient and responsive. 25. Collaboration for Results John’s history of building over 100 collaborative partnerships proves his ability to bring people together to achieve positive outcomes for the community. 26. Full-Time Commitment As mayor, John is dedicated to being a full-time leader who lives and works in Omaha, always accessible to the people he serves. 27. Support for Women’s Rights As a father, husband, and advocate for women, John will fight for women’s healthcare rights and equality in Omaha. 28. Pro-Immigrant Policies John is committed to protecting and supporting Omaha’s immigrant community, ensuring they are treated with dignity and respect. 29. Pro-Family Advocate John will implement policies that help working families thrive, ensuring that Omaha is a place where families can grow and prosper. 30. Inclusive Leadership John is a champion for inclusion, acceptance, and diversity, ensuring that all people are valued and respected in our community. 31. Retaining Young Talent John will create policies that help retain Omaha’s young people, focusing on keeping talented college graduates in the city. 32. Fighting Brain Drain John will work to reduce the brain drain in Omaha, creating initiatives that encourage college graduates to stay and contribute to our community. 33. Mentorship and Youth Advocacy John is a mentor and advocate for young people, committed to providing them with the resources and opportunities they need to succeed. 34. Focused on North and South Omaha John will be the first mayor to intentionally focus on economic development and job creation in North and South Omaha, addressing long-standing disparities. 35. Small Business Champion John recognizes that small businesses are the backbone of our economy, and he will support initiatives that foster small business growth and job creation. 36. Reflecting Omaha’s Diversity John will ensure that the diverse communities of Omaha are represented in the city’s boards and commissions, reflecting the city’s values and priorities. 37. Collaborative Economic Development John will build coalitions that create sustainable growth, reduce poverty, and strengthen our local economy. 38. Commitment to Youth John is dedicated to supporting the next generation by fostering an environment where young people can succeed and thrive in Omaha. 39. Community-Focused John’s leadership has always been about the community—listening to residents and making decisions that reflect their needs and aspirations. 40. Proven Track Record of Results John’s history of getting things done speaks for itself, from innovative public safety initiatives to improving fiscal management and increasing revenue for the county. 41. Transparency and Accountability John will bring a level of transparency and accountability to city government that ensures the people of Omaha always know what is happening and have a say in the decisions that affect them. 42. Building a Better Future for Omaha John’s vision for Omaha is one of opportunity, inclusivity, and growth—where everyone has a chance to succeed. 43. A Mayor Who Puts People First Above all, John Ewing Jr. is committed to serving the people of Omaha, ensuring that their voices are heard and their needs are met every day. Join the Campaign About John W. Ewing, Jr. As a proud Omahan, born and raised in North Omaha, I bring over four decades of public service to my role. This includes a distinguished career in the Omaha Police Department, where I retired as Deputy Chief after nearly 25 years, and 18 years of service as your Douglas County Treasurer. Beyond my professional life, I am an ordained minister at Salem Baptist Church, a devoted husband, father, and grandfather, and a respected community leader deeply rooted in Omaha. Douglas County Treasurer  During my tenure as Douglas County Treasurer, I oversaw the collection of over $1.7 billion in taxes for various governmental entities within the county. My responsibilities also included managing investments for the county and Sanitary Improvement Districts, as well as disbursing tax dollars to the appropriate entities. Deputy Chief of Police, Omaha Police Department • March 1982 – December 2006 I dedicated over 24 years to serving the citizens of Omaha as a member of the Omaha Police Department. As Deputy Chief of the Police Services Bureau, I oversaw the management of the police budget, Fleet and Facilities, the Detention Unit, Crime Lab, Research and Planning, the Customer Services Unit, the Air Support Unit, and other support services. Additionally, I served as Unit Commander of the Special Victims Unit, where I investigated crimes against women and children. I also drafted legislation to better address these crimes and testified before the Omaha City Council and the Nebraska State Legislature. As a result of this work, ten new state statutes protecting our citizens became law in the summer of 2004. Associate Minister, Salem Baptist Church • January 2002 – Present My responsibilities as Associate Minister include assisting the Pastor with leading worship services, counseling members, and visiting the sick. I also fill in for the Pastor as needed, leading Sunday services, Bible studies, programs, and special occasions. Additional duties include developing minister training and educational opportunities, teaching the Adult New Members class. I have also led other ministry areas, such as working with program ministries to plan, coordinate, and implement educational tasks and programs. I also served as an instructor for adult and youth Vacation Bible School and currently serve as an instructor for Life Development (Sunday School). John’s Vision for Omaha​ “As your future Mayor, I am resolute in my commitment to advancing key initiatives that will enhance the lives of all Omaha residents. With a wealth of experience in public service, government administration, and executive leadership, I have the vision to propel the city forward and the unwavering dedication to ensure Omaha addresses the issues that our current mayor has neglected.” Experienced Leadership Matters I will use my experience in leadership, financial expertise, management of government departments, law enforcement, community, and economic development to the table. As mayor I will support funding city services such as police, fire, street maintenance, and snow removal. I will also find innovative ways to expand our housing options for all residents and create and expand living wage jobs in our community. I will also use my expertise and experience to reduce government inefficiencies and reduce costs. A True Public Safety Mayor for Omaha As a 25-year veteran of the Omaha Police Department, including my tenure as Deputy Police Chief, I have an unwavering commitment to the safety of our community. I am deeply concerned by the department’s recent struggles with recruitment and retention. During my time with the Omaha Police Department, I successfully doubled the number of qualified and diverse recruits, and I am eager to apply this experience to address the current staffing challenges. Furthermore, I am dedicated to implementing comprehensive strategies that will ensure the safety of our city. These strategies include a focus on community policing, proper officer training, and clear accountability standards that protect both officers and the public. I firmly believe that these measures will create a safer Omaha for all residents. Budding Economic Development My administration is committed to fostering economic growth and creating opportunities for all. We will actively work to attract new businesses, support local entrepreneurs, and invest in infrastructure projects that will stimulate economic development and job creation. To achieve these goals, we will partner with the business community, the Omaha Chamber of Commerce, educational institutions, and school districts to improve our workforce. We recognize the educational challenges facing our city, with its diverse population speaking over 100 languages. We are committed to addressing these challenges collaboratively to ensure a brighter future for all the people of Omaha. Strengthening Infrastructure and Housing My administration is committed to improving the quality of our city’s infrastructure and addressing the urgent housing crisis. To ensure our streets are paved to the highest standard, we will engage experts who specialize in concrete mix design for our specific climate. We will support our Public Works staff by providing them with the resources and expertise necessary to effectively maintain our streets, with a focus on equitable distribution of repairs throughout Omaha. Additionally, we recognize the critical shortage of high-density affordable housing. My administration will prioritize the addition of at least 1,500 affordable housing units to our housing stock. This initiative will not only address the immediate housing shortage but also create living-wage jobs. Increasing Fiscal Responsibility I have a deep understanding of the critical importance of sound financial management. As Mayor, I will be dedicated to working closely with government department leaders and staff to ensure that taxpayer dollars are used responsibly and efficiently, while maintaining the highest standards of accountability across all areas of city government. Increasing Government Transparency and Accountability As an elected official I value an open and transparent government. My administration will implement measures to enhance transparency and make it easier for residents to access information about city operations, budgets, and the decision-making processes. I will ensure that major decisions impacting the financial future of our city are put to a vote of the electorate. Retaining Our Youth I am committed to fostering a thriving community that attracts and retains young professionals and families. My administration will prioritize initiatives that support education, workforce development, and affordable housing to achieve this goal. We will leverage the pipeline created by the Nebraska University System’s all-expense paid scholarships for high-achieving students and build strong partnerships with local educational institutions, including UNO, Heider College, UNMC, Clarkson, and Methodist Schools of Nursing. By collaborating with these institutions, we can ensure that our city’s best and brightest choose to stay and contribute to our community. Improving Omaha’s Reputation My substantial involvement in economic development and community service organizations reflects my dedication to improving Omaha’s reputation and quality of life. I firmly believe that Omaha’s reputation will be significantly enhanced by addressing key areas such as street maintenance, housing availability, and economic development opportunities. Join Our Campaign Donate Today! John W. Ewing, Jr. Political Candidate for Omaha Mayor (402) 261-0624 cerberus.strategies@gmail.com PO Box 541034, Omaha, NE 68154 Paid for by John Ewing for Omaha | All rights reserved.

Tara Reardon for Senate New Hampshire District 15 Senate State Senate District 15 deserves a strong voice that comes from experience, years of relationship-building and proven success.  I want to be that Senator for you.  I pledge to bring all my experience, intellect and common sense to the job. ​ I am honored and humbled to have the endorsement of Congresswoman Annie Kuster, Governor John Lynch and Senator Sylvia Larsen.   These seasoned public servants know what it takes to do the job and have confidence in me to be the next State Senator for District 15. ​ The Capital Region faces unique challenges in the upcoming years: a new men’s prison, the widening of I-93, a new parking garage in our downtown and the demotion of an old one, and a forensic hospital in a residential neighborhood.  I have strong proven relationships at the state, county and local level to make residents' concerns heard, and I will listen and represent those voices. DONATE Senate Priorities I am dedicated to protecting women’s health care rights, addressing our housing crisis with 30 years of expertise, supporting our public schools, and championing affordable clean energy for all. Together, we can build a brighter, healthier future for New Hampshire. What Community Leaders are Saying Congresswoman Annie Kuster "I have known Tara Reardon since we met in seventh grade and we have shared a passion for public service ever since. As young teenagers, Tara and I would watch the N.H. Legislature from the gallery and discuss the issues of the day. Tara has dedicated her life to the challenges facing hard working New Hampshire families, from access to affordable housing and child care to preventing domestic violence and supporting survivors. She has the courage and conviction to tackle tough issues and improve the quality of life for all in N.H." State Representative Dave Luneau "I have worked with Tara and can confidently say she is a problem-solver with unparalleled governmental experience.  Tara’s vision for our community, and proven track record make her the best choice for Senate District 15,  I wholeheartedly support her candidacy and encourage you to do the same." Governor John Lynch "As Governor of NH, I worked hard to strengthen our public schools, protect our most vulnerable, and create good paying jobs. I couldn’t do this without Tara Reardon, and I am excited to endorse her for State Senate in our district to apply her hands-on experience of delivering results for hard-working Granite Staters.  Tara will be an outstanding State Senator." State Representative Mel Myler "Tara is the quintessential public servant.  Her life has been focused on serving the public by listening, learning and acting in the interests of her constituents.  Her years of commitment at the local, state and county level gives her a unique perspective to the needs of the state.  She knows how to find the common ground on hard issues.  As a State Senator, she will improve the lives of hard working Granite Staters." Senator Sylvia Larsen "I enthusiastically endorse Tara Reardon for State Senate in District 15. Throughout my tenure, I worked diligently for the people of this district and I am confident that Tara is the right person to continue this work.  Whether working on housing, public education, women’s health, or veterans issues, Tara has shown a deep commitment to our community, a clear vision for our future, and the integrity and leadership we need in the State Senate.  I encourage the voters of Concord , Hopkinton and Bow to join me in supporting Tara Reardon for State Senate." Mayor Byron Champlin “Tara Reardon has deep knowledge of municipal government, a keen understanding of how the state works and a track record of effective relationship building between the two. These are skills we need in our next Senator and just three of the many reasons I’m endorsing Tara for State Senate.“ DONATE Strong Leadership Support strong, experienced leadership—your donation today ensures we can tackle our community's challenges and create a brighter future for all. Donate Today Join Team Reardon Get involved in the campaign. Standing up to big developers and special interests to deliver for Jersey City’s Working Families. JOIN AS VOLUNTEER Make a difference in your community. Join us now Join us now James Solomon × As a dad, a cancer survivor, an educator, and a Councilmember, James Solomon has given back to Jersey City—helping keep thousands of tenants in their homes, protecting small businesses, and uplifting real democracy. But our city is at a crossroads. As our next Mayor, James will take on developers and self-interested insiders to deliver results: Building more affordable housing to help longtime residents stay in their homes Making developers pay their fair share to produce more affordability, better public  schools, and more open spaces—all while making their donations public and auditing their tax breaks to hold them to their promises Cracking down on insider politics that costs taxpayers money and hurts city services Working with law enforcement to keep our streets safe, and creating more after-schools and summer jobs programs Working to restart PATH service. unprocessed_text: Skip to main content Main navigation About Andrew Issues Press Releases Get Involved Buttons menu Donate Ready on Day One Safety, Affordability, Opportunity for All SAY NO TO ZO Featured Public Safety A safe New York City will be the first priority of the Cuomo Administration and is a precondition to achieve the progress our city must make in other areas. Read the plan Andrew Cuomo for Mayor of New York City Andrew Cuomo Unveils Aggressive Plan to Bolster NYPD Ranks, Morale Working for YOU Affordability New York City faces a crisis of affordability. Andrew Cuomo’s plans for housing, childcare, health, transportation, economic development, and job creation, will bring down the cost of living for New Yorkers. Public Safety A safe New York City will be the first priority of the Cuomo Administration. In order for NYC to make progress, New Yorkers and visitors to the city must first be and feel safe. Subway Safety Making the subways safe and restoring riders’ confidence requires a comprehensive approach that is implemented with consistent enforcement and strong accountability. Housing As Mayor, Andrew Cuomo will accelerate the pace of housing construction and will protect the rights of tenants and keep families from falling into homelessness. Education and Childcare After public safety, the Mayor of New York City has no higher calling than ensuring that the students enrolled in the public school system receive a high-quality education. Health and Wellness Improving the health of all New Yorkers is a priority that includes not only traditional physical healthcare, but also mental healthcare. A Clean and Livable New York City Creating a clean and livable city is essential to improving the quality of life for all New Yorkers and restoring pride in our public spaces. Combating Antisemitism The rise of antisemitism across the world, in our nation and even here in New York City, is repulsive and will be met with all the legal and moral force needed to ensure that no New Yorker feels victimized or threatened. Economic Development The key to economic development and job creation is to ensure that New York City adopts policies that are conducive to the business community and inclusive of all New Yorkers. Mental Health, Addiction, & Homelessness Revamping the mental health and substance use disorder care systems will reduce hospitalizations, homelessness, and incarceration. Read the plans Fighting for NYC Andrew Cuomo is dedicated to New York and to public service, having served as U.S. Secretary of Housing and Urban Development, Attorney General of New York, and as New York’s 56th Governor. He has the leadership skills and experience to make government work for the people of New York City. unprocessed_text: Skip to main content Main navigation About Andrew Issues Press Releases Get Involved Buttons menu Donate On the Issues Education and Childcare After public safety, the Mayor of New York City has no higher calling than ensuring that the more than 900,000 students enrolled in the New York City public school system receive a high-quality education. The sheer size of the school system makes mayoral control an imperative to ensure accountability for performance. A commitment to excellence and high standards must go hand-in-hand with adequate resources. Andrew Cuomo’s education plan will: Reduce class size.  Secure state funding to implement the Reduced Class Size law so it is not an unfunded mandate. Ensure the Reduced Class Size law does not reduce the capacity of selective schools. Guarantee a suitable 3-K slot to all families and expand after school programs.  Ensure sufficient 3-K programs are available to offer slots aligned with where families live and work. Strengthen Community Schools as a hub for social services in high poverty districts.  Increase the number of School-Based Health Centers with a goal of having a SBHC in every Community School. Address chronic absenteeism and teacher attrition.  The current 35% chronic absenteeism rate (more than 50% in certain areas) is unacceptable and must be fixed, beginning with stronger engagement with parents. To reduce teacher attrition, we need better working conditions, improved safety, and enhanced mentoring. Strengthen career and technical education (CTE).  CTE programs are a key pathway to economic mobility. Partner with businesses and develop apprenticeship programs to develop good paying jobs for students who don’t have college degrees. Read More Affordability New York City faces a crisis of affordability. Our City has always been expensive, but today it is rapidly becoming unaffordable for millions of hard-working New Yorkers at all income levels. Andrew Cuomo’s plans for New York City, including those addressing housing, childcare, health, transportation, economic development, and job creation, will make the cost of living more affordable for New Yorkers. Andrew Cuomo’s affordability plan for New York City will: Increase the supply of affordable housing. Expand access to affordable healthcare.  Connect all New Yorkers to primary care and specialty care when needed. Assist New Yorkers who are denied coverage by their insurance plan. Guarantee universal 3-K and increase childcare options. Make transportation more affordable  by expanding discounted access to buses and significantly increasing the percentage of New Yorkers who enroll in the subsidized Fair Fares programs for which they are eligible. Provide targeted tax relief to lower income and middle-class voters,  including homeowners. Improve incomes  through job creation, workforce training, and worker protections and via tax cutting measures. Read More Public Safety A safe New York City will be the first priority of the Cuomo Administration. In order for NYC to make progress, New Yorkers and visitors to the city must first be and feel safe. Andrew Cuomo’s plan for public safety will: Increase the size of the NYPD.  Andrew Cuomo will add 5,000 new police officers to the force—quickly—bringing the ranks of the NYPD up to 39,000, the size of the force 25 years ago.  Offer bonuses and incentives to active and recently retired police officers  to boost morale, increase officer retention, and make the NYPD more competitive with other large city police forces' salaries. Deploy police officers based on proven, data-driven strategies,  focusing on recidivists who are responsible for a disproportionate share of crime and on the locations where most crime occurs. Crack down on nuisance and quality of life crimes.  Tackle retail theft, identify habitual offenders, disrupt fencing operations, and increase enforcement. Increase accountability for e-bike and moped violations  by beginning traffic enforcement that has been absent, and holding businesses responsible for infractions. Read More Subway Safety Subway safety is essential. To make the subways safe and to restore rider confidence, a comprehensive approach will be implemented with consistent enforcement and strong accountability. Andrew Cuomo’s subway safety plan will: Increase permanent presence and deployment of NYPD and MTA officers  in stations and on subways. Add substantially more officers to NYPD Transit to reduce mandatory overtime while restoring the force level to match the scale of the task. The police presence will be robust and respectful, ensuring rider safety and confidence. Prevent unlawful entry through better infrastructure.  Upgrade turnstiles across all stations and improve training to prevent fare evasion. Work with District Attorneys to address crimes on our subways, including vandalism, drug use, and disorderly conduct. Increase outreach to homeless individuals on subways.  Partner with mental health services, shelters, and diversion centers to provide alternatives for those removed from the subway system. Read More Housing New York City is in a housing crisis. The existing supply of housing is increasingly unaffordable and its quality is deteriorating. As Mayor, Andrew Cuomo will use every lever at the City’s disposal to accelerate the pace of construction of affordable and workforce housing, while protecting the rights of tenants and keeping families from falling into homelessness. Andrew Cuomo’s plan to address Housing will: Build more housing across all income levels.  Increasing the supply of housing is essential to stabilizing and ultimately reducing rents, as well as creating the workforce housing that cannot be built by the private sector alone. Leverage public land for housing development.  Identify and redevelop underutilized city-owned lots for affordable and mixed-income housing. Improve the quality of life in  NYCHA Housing .  We can improve the quality of life in NYCHA Housing while working with tenants to explore redevelopment in a way that increases the supply of housing. We will include resident input and leadership in every decision we make regarding NYCHA. Protect renters and keep families in their homes.  Enhance enforcement of rent-stabilization laws to prevent illegal rent hikes and landlord harassment. Ensure that every tenant facing eviction has counsel in housing court to reduce evictions that result in homelessness. Read More Mental Health, Addiction, & Homelessness Many low-level violations and crimes on our streets and subways are attributable to individuals who are seriously mentally ill or suffering from addiction, many of whom are homeless. The status quo acceptance of this as a condition of urban life is not compassionate. It leaves the suffering of these individuals untreated and diminishes the quality of life in New York City. Fixing the many flaws in the current mental health and substance use disorder care system will reduce hospitalizations, homelessness, and incarceration among individuals with serious mental illness or substance use disorders, while improving the quality of life for all New Yorkers. Andrew Cuomo’s plan to address mental health and homelessness will: Expand access to mental health and substance use disorder services , particularly for the homeless, with a focus on community-based services. Meet the mental health needs of children and adolescents.  Prioritize schools as key access points for mental health services. Restructure inpatient psychiatric bed capacity  to end the revolving door and enable longer stays, particularly for those without housing security. Consistently enforce involuntary commitment laws.  Codify in statute the existing standard that permits involuntarily committing individuals who are a danger to themselves because they cannot meet their basic needs. Ensure that judges, prosecutors, and defense lawyers consistently seek Article 730 mental health competency exams for defendants whose offenses are related to their serious mental illness. Reduce chronic street homelessness.  Focus on outreach to the roughly 2,000 chronically homeless people who need intensive mental health and addiction services to bring them in from the streets and subways. Read More Health and Wellness New York City is home to some of the best health care services in the world, but access to these services is not evenly distributed among its residents. The large disparities in health outcomes by race and neighborhood are striking. Improving the health of all New Yorkers is a priority that includes not only traditional physical healthcare, but also mental healthcare. In addition, substance use disorder assistance, public health measures such as vaccinations, and addressing the social determinants of health, such as housing and healthy food, are necessary for a healthy population. Andrew Cuomo’s health and wellness plan will: Ensure that all New Yorkers have access to primary care and specialty care when needed.  Build on the strengths of Health + Hospitals, including its NYC Cares program, and partner with federally qualified community health centers and large independent physician associations that are providing community-based care in their neighborhoods. Make healthcare more convenient in hard-to-serve communities  by bringing services to more schools and public housing. Reduce health disparities.  Establish targeted programs with concrete performance goals to reduce health disparities in maternal health and chronic diseases such as diabetes and asthma. Strengthen safety net hospitals.  Create stronger collaboration among Health + Hospitals, private safety net hospitals, and large academic medical centers. Protect public health.  New York City must be more focused than ever on core public health functions – pandemic preparedness, childhood vaccinations, environmental health – in the face of a federal government that is failing to address these challenges. Integrate physical health and behavioral health.  Encourage co-location of services and seamless exchange of healthcare information to address the growing problems of mental health and substance use disorders. Combating Antisemitism Our New York values demand respect for all and intimidation of none. The rise of antisemitism across the world, in our nation, and even here in New York City, is repulsive and will be met with all the legal and moral force needed to ensure that no New Yorker feels victimized or threatened. Andrew Cuomo's plan to combat antisemitism will: Ensure that those who threaten, harass, or destroy property in the name of antisemitism are held accountable. Provide a strong response to antisemitic incidents in schools,  including curriculum reforms. Adopt the International Holocaust Remembrance Association (IHRA) definition of antisemitism  to ensure that city officials and law enforcement are battling antisemitism in all forms. A Clean and Livable New York City New Yorkers deserve a city that is safe, affordable and well-maintained. In order for families to thrive, businesses to grow, and residents to enjoy their neighborhoods, New York City must take steps to improve the cleanliness and order of public spaces. Andrew Cuomo’s plan will: End open air drug markets and use.  Enforce state laws which allow people who appear to be incapacitated by alcohol and/or drugs, and may be a danger to themselves or others, to be taken to treatment facilities for emergency services. Curb the overuse of sidewalk sheds (scaffolding)  with legislation that limits the time that scaffolding can remain in place. Amend regulations to allow safe alternatives to scaffolding. Streamline building inspection timelines, speed up Local Law 11 permitting, and incentivize building owners to take down scaffolding quickly. Improve curbside composting.  Boost participation in curbside composting programs through public awareness campaigns, incentives and resources for landlords, and clear guidelines and training. Work with large buildings to make implementation easier and more effective for residents and building staff. Reform the outdoor dining permit process.  Clear the backlog of thousands of applicants waiting for outdoor dining licenses by adding resources that will: provide transparency to help establishments navigate the outdoor dining process efficiently; set clear timelines for permitting decisions, allowing small businesses to plan outdoor dining setups and staffing; improve coordination among city agencies with jurisdiction over outdoor dining permits; and set and enforce standards of cleanliness and security for outdoor dining. Economic Development The key to economic development and job creation is to ensure that New York City adopts policies that are conducive to the business community and inclusive of all New Yorkers. Job creation produces tax revenue which can be invested in services that improve quality of life which in turn leads to more job creation. Andrew Cuomo’s economic development and job creation plan will: Strengthen education and workforce development for economic mobility.  Expand workforce training and apprenticeships, particularly in nontraditional fields like healthcare, to create more pathways to economic mobility. Fix the K-12 education system to increase both college attainment and better preparation for jobs that don’t require a college degree. Promote inclusive economic growth and job creation.  Expand wage supplements and other programs targeted to at-risk men (ages 18-24) to help them enter the workforce. Protect Minority- and Women-Owned Business Enterprise (MWBE) programs. Drive economic growth through infrastructure investment.  Expand the City’s role in major infrastructure projects and prioritize infrastructure projects in the outer boroughs, such as the Metro-North expansion that will create four new transit hubs in the East Bronx. Revitalize the City’s central business districts.  Support office-to-residential conversions, especially for underused commercial properties and transform business districts into 24/7 destinations. Build up sectors where New York City has a competitive advantage.  With its world-leading hospitals and strong presence in technology, New York City can become a leader in Artificial Intelligence (AI), which will be the dominant economic force in the decades to come. Andrew Cuomos subway safety plan will: Increase permanent presence and deployment of NYPD and MTA officers  in stations and on subways. Add substantially more officers to NYPD Transit to reduce mandatory overtime while restoring the force level to match the scale of the task. The police presence will be robust and respectful, ensuring rider safety and confidence. Prevent unlawful entry through better infrastructure.  Upgrade turnstiles across all stations and improve training to prevent fare evasion. Work with District Attorneys to address crimes on our subways, including vandalism, drug use, and disorderly conduct. Increase outreach to homeless individuals on subways.  Partner with mental health services, shelters, and diversion centers to provide alternatives for those removed from the subway system. Read More Housing New York City is in a housing crisis. The existing supply of housing is increasingly unaffordable and its quality is deteriorating. As Mayor, Andrew Cuomo will use every lever at the Citys disposal to accelerate the pace of construction of affordable and workforce housing, while protecting the rights of tenants and keeping families from falling into homelessness. Andrew Cuomos plan to address Housing will: Build more housing across all income levels.  Increasing the supply of housing is essential to stabilizing and ultimately reducing rents, as well as creating the workforce housing that cannot be built by the private sector alone. Leverage public land for housing development.  Identify and redevelop underutilized city-owned lots for affordable and mixed-income housing. Improve the quality of life in  NYCHA Housing .  We can improve the quality of life in NYCHA Housing while working with tenants to explore redevelopment in a way that increases the supply of housing. We will include resident input and leadership in every decision we make regarding NYCHA. Protect renters and keep families in their homes.  Enhance enforcement of rent-stabilization laws to prevent illegal rent hikes and landlord harassment. Ensure that every tenant facing eviction has counsel in housing court to reduce evictions that result in homelessness. Read More Mental Health, Addiction, & Homelessness Many low-level violations and crimes on our streets and subways are attributable to individuals who are seriously mentally ill or suffering from addiction, many of whom are homeless. The status quo acceptance of this as a condition of urban life is not compassionate. It leaves the suffering of these individuals untreated and diminishes the quality of life in New York City. Fixing the many flaws in the current mental health and substance use disorder care system will reduce hospitalizations, homelessness, and incarceration among individuals with serious mental illness or substance use disorders, while improving the quality of life for all New Yorkers. Andrew Cuomos plan to address mental health and homelessness will: Expand access to mental health and substance use disorder services , particularly for the homeless, with a focus on community-based services. Meet the mental health needs of children and adolescents.  Prioritize schools as key access points for mental health services. Restructure inpatient psychiatric bed capacity  to end the revolving door and enable longer stays, particularly for those without housing security. Consistently enforce involuntary commitment laws.  Codify in statute the existing standard that permits involuntarily committing individuals who are a danger to themselves because they cannot meet their basic needs. Ensure that judges, prosecutors, and defense lawyers consistently seek Article 730 mental health competency exams for defendants whose offenses are related to their serious mental illness. Reduce chronic street homelessness.  Focus on outreach to the roughly 2,000 chronically homeless people who need intensive mental health and addiction services to bring them in from the streets and subways. Read More Health and Wellness New York City is home to some of the best health care services in the world, but access to these services is not evenly distributed among its residents. The large disparities in health outcomes by race and neighborhood are striking. Improving the health of all New Yorkers is a priority that includes not only traditional physical healthcare, but also mental healthcare. In addition, substance use disorder assistance, public health measures such as vaccinations, and addressing the social determinants of health, such as housing and healthy food, are necessary for a healthy population. Andrew Cuomos health and wellness plan will: Ensure that all New Yorkers have access to primary care and specialty care when needed.  Build on the strengths of Health  Hospitals, including its NYC Cares program, and partner with federally qualified community health centers and large independent physician associations that are providing community-based care in their neighborhoods. Make healthcare more convenient in hard-to-serve communities  by bringing services to more schools and public housing. Reduce health disparities.  Establish targeted programs with concrete performance goals to reduce health disparities in maternal health and chronic diseases such as diabetes and asthma. Strengthen safety net hospitals.  Create stronger collaboration among Health  Hospitals, private safety net hospitals, and large academic medical centers. Protect public health.  New York City must be more focused than ever on core public health functions  pandemic preparedness, childhood vaccinations, environmental health  in the face of a federal government that is failing to address these challenges. Integrate physical health and behavioral health.  Encourage co-location of services and seamless exchange of healthcare information to address the growing problems of mental health and substance use disorders. Combating Antisemitism Our New York values demand respect for all and intimidation of none. The rise of antisemitism across the world, in our nation, and even here in New York City, is repulsive and will be met with all the legal and moral force needed to ensure that no New Yorker feels victimized or threatened. Andrew Cuomo's plan to combat antisemitism will: Ensure that those who threaten, harass, or destroy property in the name of antisemitism are held accountable. Provide a strong response to antisemitic incidents in schools,  including curriculum reforms. Adopt the International Holocaust Remembrance Association (IHRA) definition of antisemitism  to ensure that city officials and law enforcement are battling antisemitism in all forms. A Clean and Livable New York City New Yorkers deserve a city that is safe, affordable and well-maintained. In order for families to thrive, businesses to grow, and residents to enjoy their neighborhoods, New York City must take steps to improve the cleanliness and order of public spaces. Andrew Cuomos plan will: End open air drug markets and use.  Enforce state laws which allow people who appear to be incapacitated by alcohol andor drugs, and may be a danger to themselves or others, to be taken to treatment facilities for emergency services. Curb the overuse of sidewalk sheds (scaffolding)  with legislation that limits the time that scaffolding can remain in place. Amend regulations to allow safe alternatives to scaffolding. Streamline building inspection timelines, speed up Local Law 11 permitting, and incentivize building owners to take down scaffolding quickly. Improve curbside composting.  Boost participation in curbside composting programs through public awareness campaigns, incentives and resources for landlords, and clear guidelines and training. Work with large buildings to make implementation easier and more effective for residents and building staff. Reform the outdoor dining permit process.  Clear the backlog of thousands of applicants waiting for outdoor dining licenses by adding resources that will: provide transparency to help establishments navigate the outdoor dining process efficiently set clear timelines for permitting decisions, allowing small businesses to plan outdoor dining setups and staffing improve coordination among city agencies with jurisdiction over outdoor dining permits and set and enforce standards of cleanliness and security for outdoor dining. Economic Development The key to economic development and job creation is to ensure that New York City adopts policies that are conducive to the business community and inclusive of all New Yorkers. Job creation produces tax revenue which can be invested in services that improve quality of life which in turn leads to more job creation. Andrew Cuomos economic development and job creation plan will: Strengthen education and workforce development for economic mobility.  Expand workforce training and apprenticeships, particularly in nontraditional fields like healthcare, to create more pathways to economic mobility. Fix the K-12 education system to increase both college attainment and better preparation for jobs that dont require a college degree. Promote inclusive economic growth and job creation.  Expand wage supplements and other programs targeted to at-risk men (ages 18-24) to help them enter the workforce. Protect Minority- and Women-Owned Business Enterprise (MWBE) programs. Drive economic growth through infrastructure investment.  Expand the Citys role in major infrastructure projects and prioritize infrastructure projects in the outer boroughs, such as the Metro-North expansion that will create four new transit hubs in the East Bronx. Revitalize the Citys central business districts.  Support office-to-residential conversions, especially for underused commercial properties and transform business districts into 247 destinations. Build up sectors where New York City has a competitive advantage.  With its world-leading hospitals and strong presence in technology, New York City can become a leader in Artificial Intelligence (AI), which will be the dominant economic force in the decades to come. unprocessed_text: Skip to main content Main navigation About Andrew Issues Press Releases Get Involved Buttons menu Donate On the Issues Education and Childcare After public safety, the Mayor of New York City has no higher calling than ensuring that the more than 900,000 students enrolled in the New York City public school system receive a high-quality education. The sheer size of the school system makes mayoral control an imperative to ensure accountability for performance. A commitment to excellence and high standards must go hand-in-hand with adequate resources. Andrew Cuomo’s education plan will: Reduce class size.  Secure state funding to implement the Reduced Class Size law so it is not an unfunded mandate. Ensure the Reduced Class Size law does not reduce the capacity of selective schools. Guarantee a suitable 3-K slot to all families and expand after school programs.  Ensure sufficient 3-K programs are available to offer slots aligned with where families live and work. Strengthen Community Schools as a hub for social services in high poverty districts.  Increase the number of School-Based Health Centers with a goal of having a SBHC in every Community School. Address chronic absenteeism and teacher attrition.  The current 35% chronic absenteeism rate (more than 50% in certain areas) is unacceptable and must be fixed, beginning with stronger engagement with parents. To reduce teacher attrition, we need better working conditions, improved safety, and enhanced mentoring. Strengthen career and technical education (CTE).  CTE programs are a key pathway to economic mobility. Partner with businesses and develop apprenticeship programs to develop good paying jobs for students who don’t have college degrees. Read More Affordability New York City faces a crisis of affordability. Our City has always been expensive, but today it is rapidly becoming unaffordable for millions of hard-working New Yorkers at all income levels. Andrew Cuomo’s plans for New York City, including those addressing housing, childcare, health, transportation, economic development, and job creation, will make the cost of living more affordable for New Yorkers. Andrew Cuomo’s affordability plan for New York City will: Increase the supply of affordable housing. Expand access to affordable healthcare.  Connect all New Yorkers to primary care and specialty care when needed. Assist New Yorkers who are denied coverage by their insurance plan. Guarantee universal 3-K and increase childcare options. Make transportation more affordable  by expanding discounted access to buses and significantly increasing the percentage of New Yorkers who enroll in the subsidized Fair Fares programs for which they are eligible. Provide targeted tax relief to lower income and middle-class voters,  including homeowners. Improve incomes  through job creation, workforce training, and worker protections and via tax cutting measures. Read More Public Safety A safe New York City will be the first priority of the Cuomo Administration. In order for NYC to make progress, New Yorkers and visitors to the city must first be and feel safe. Andrew Cuomo’s plan for public safety will: Increase the size of the NYPD. Offer bonuses and incentives to active and recently retired police officers  to boost morale, increase officer retention, and make the NYPD more competitive with other large city police forces' salaries. Deploy police officers based on proven, data-driven strategies,  focusing on recidivists who are responsible for a disproportionate share of crime and on the locations where most crime occurs. Crack down on nuisance and quality of life crimes.  Tackle retail theft, identify habitual offenders, disrupt fencing operations, and increase enforcement. Increase accountability for e-bike and moped violations  by beginning traffic enforcement that has been absent, and holding businesses responsible for infractions. Build on the strengths of Health  Hospitals, including its NYC Cares program, and partner with federally qualified community health centers and large independent physician associations that are providing community-based care in their neighborhoods. Make healthcare more convenient in hard-to-serve communities  by bringing services to more schools and public housing. Reduce health disparities.  Establish targeted programs with concrete performance goals to reduce health disparities in maternal health and chronic diseases such as diabetes and asthma. Strengthen safety net hospitals.  Create stronger collaboration among Health  Hospitals, private safety net hospitals, and large academic medical centers. Protect public health.  New York City must be more focused than ever on core public health functions  pandemic preparedness, childhood vaccinations, environmental health  in the face of a federal government that is failing to address these challenges. Integrate physical health and behavioral health.  Encourage co-location of services and seamless exchange of healthcare information to address the growing problems of mental health and substance use disorders. Combating Antisemitism Our New York values demand respect for all and intimidation of none. The rise of antisemitism across the world, in our nation, and even here in New York City, is repulsive and will be met with all the legal and moral force needed to ensure that no New Yorker feels victimized or threatened. Andrew Cuomo's plan to combat antisemitism will: Ensure that those who threaten, harass, or destroy property in the name of antisemitism are held accountable. Provide a strong response to antisemitic incidents in schools,  including curriculum reforms. Adopt the International Holocaust Remembrance Association (IHRA) definition of antisemitism  to ensure that city officials and law enforcement are battling antisemitism in all forms. A Clean and Livable New York City New Yorkers deserve a city that is safe, affordable and well-maintained. In order for families to thrive, businesses to grow, and residents to enjoy their neighborhoods, New York City must take steps to improve the cleanliness and order of public spaces. Andrew Cuomos plan will: End open air drug markets and use.  Enforce state laws which allow people who appear to be incapacitated by alcohol andor drugs, and may be a danger to themselves or others, to be taken to treatment facilities for emergency services. Curb the overuse of sidewalk sheds (scaffolding)  with legislation that limits the time that scaffolding can remain in place. Amend regulations to allow safe alternatives to scaffolding. Streamline building inspection timelines, speed up Local Law 11 permitting, and incentivize building owners to take down scaffolding quickly. Improve curbside composting.  Boost participation in curbside composting programs through public awareness campaigns, incentives and resources for landlords, and clear guidelines and training. Work with large buildings to make implementation easier and more effective for residents and building staff. Reform the outdoor dining permit process.  Clear the backlog of thousands of applicants waiting for outdoor dining licenses by adding resources that will: provide transparency to help establishments navigate the outdoor dining process efficiently set clear timelines for permitting decisions, allowing small businesses to plan outdoor dining setups and staffing improve coordination among city agencies with jurisdiction over outdoor dining permits and set and enforce standards of cleanliness and security for outdoor dining. Economic Development The key to economic development and job creation is to ensure that New York City adopts policies that are conducive to the business community and inclusive of all New Yorkers. Job creation produces tax revenue which can be invested in services that improve quality of life which in turn leads to more job creation. Andrew Cuomos economic development and job creation plan will: Strengthen education and workforce development for economic mobility.  Expand workforce training and apprenticeships, particularly in nontraditional fields like healthcare, to create more pathways to economic mobility. Fix the K-12 education system to increase both college attainment and better preparation for jobs that dont require a college degree. Promote inclusive economic growth and job creation.  Expand wage supplements and other programs targeted to at-risk men (ages 18-24) to help them enter the workforce. Protect Minority- and Women-Owned Business Enterprise (MWBE) programs. Drive economic growth through infrastructure investment.  Expand the Citys role in major infrastructure projects and prioritize infrastructure projects in the outer boroughs, such as the Metro-North expansion that will create four new transit hubs in the East Bronx. Revitalize the Citys central business districts.  Support office-to-residential conversions, especially for underused commercial properties and transform business districts into 247 destinations. Build up sectors where New York City has a competitive advantage.  With its world-leading hospitals and strong presence in technology, New York City can become a leader in Artificial Intelligence (AI), which will be the dominant economic force in the decades to come.
